title: Welcome to the Deep Learning (DL) & AI Course!

content.problemStatement:
Get ready to unlock the power of machines that can **see, hear, and even understand patterns**! In this course, you’ll explore the fascinating world of **Deep Learning (DL), Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Large Language Models (LLMs)**, and learn how to apply them to **real-world problems**.



**Prerequisites:**
Before you start, make sure you are comfortable with:
- **Python programming**, **NumPy** (for numerical operations) and **Pandas** (for data manipulation)
- **Matplotlib/Seaborn** for visualization
- **Basic Machine Learning concepts** (classification, regression, clustering)

---


**How This Course Works**

- Bite-sized lessons for **easy learning at your own pace**
- **Visual explanations** to make complex concepts simple and intuitive
- **Learn Deep Learning step by step** from neurons and MLPs to architectures, activations, optimizers, and how DL differs from ML.
- **Build real projects across domains** like Handwritten Digit Recognition, Cat vs Dog Image Classification, and text tasks like hate speech detection.
- **Real-world examples** to understand why DL and AI matter

**Advanced Applications:**
- Turning **images and text into numerical features**
- **Feature engineering** for sequences and time series
- **Text classification, sentiment analysis, summarization, and chatbots**
- Explore the relationship between **AI, ML, DL, and LLMs** and understand **how large language models work**


**Click “Next” to start your Deep Learning adventure!**
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
